en.name: Ventilation mode - Spontaneous/support
en.shortDescription: Spontaneous and support modes (pressure support, CPAP, bilevel/BiPAP) — the patient triggers breaths, the ventilator assists.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Spontaneous and support ventilation modes, where the patient triggers each breath and the ventilator assists rather than imposing a mandatory rate — pressure support (PSV), continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) and bilevel/dual-pressure support (BiPAP-type). In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating the ventilation mode in use; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. The mode is independent of whether ventilation is invasive (via an endotracheal/tracheostomy tube) or non-invasive (via a mask) — that distinction is captured in the Invasive mechanical ventilation and Non-invasive ventilation concept sets.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED procedure concepts for these modes.

- *3661883 / 34291000175108 — Nocturnal dual pressure spontaneous ventilation support (SNOMED)*
- *4055262 / 243142003 — Dual pressure spontaneous ventilation support (SNOMED)*
- *4165535 / 47545007 — Continuous positive airway pressure ventilation treatment (SNOMED)*
- *37152400 / 1186731003 — Continuous spontaneous ventilation with pressure support (SNOMED)*
- *37152401 / 1186732005 — Continuous spontaneous ventilation with volume targeted pressure-support (SNOMED)*
- *37152402 / 1186733000 — Continuous spontaneous ventilation with effort support (SNOMED)*
- *37154096 / 1259864003 — Pressure support ventilation (SNOMED)*
- *40491848 / 447243000 — Bilevel positive airway pressure titration (SNOMED)*

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded ventilation mode to the matching concept here based on the control variable / mode family, regardless of whether the patient is invasively or non-invasively ventilated.
- **Mode vs ventilation act**: this set captures the *mode* only; whether the patient is on invasive or non-invasive ventilation is recorded separately (Invasive mechanical ventilation / Non-invasive ventilation concept sets).
